coach_insights.commentary:
BIOMECHANICS: First rally of the clip, and Player A's profile is tight. Court Position sits at just 0.29 m behind the baseline — he's holding territory, not defending. Court Coverage peaked at only 0.26 m/s lateral — no side-to-side stress applied. Recovery Lag logged a single 33 ms reading on rally exit (match_coupling), which is a transition artifact, not fatigue. No baseline exists yet for z-scoring.

STRATEGY: Stay aggressive on court position — Player A's 0.29 m retreat and 0.26 m/s Court Coverage show this rally cost him nothing. With no lateral load banked, he can afford to step in on the next return and take time away. Treat the next 2-3 rallies as baseline-building: keep Court Position under 0.5 m and push shape early, before any Recovery Lag signature emerges.

NARRATIVE: Player A just walked through that rally — zero defensive footprint, tank is full, time to press.

BIOMECHANICS: Player A just closed his third micro-rally in 13 seconds (rally boundaries at 11033, 12300, 13333 ms, all match_coupling). Court Position spiked mid-sequence — Player A sat 0.78 m behind the baseline on rally two before resetting to 0.12 m on rally three. Court Coverage is suppressed: 95th-pct lateral velocity of 0.09–0.26 m/s with a negative trend slope (-0.072 m/s per sec). These are exchange-only bursts, not real rallies.

STRATEGY: Too early for fatigue calls — no baseline yet. But the 0.78 m retreat on rally two is a red flag in a 1-second exchange; Player A is bailing backwards before he's even being pressured. Hold the +0.10 m Court Position line through the next serve window. Court Coverage at 0.26 m/s ceiling means he isn't being run yet — stay aggressive on contact, don't pre-concede depth.

NARRATIVE: Player A's ceding a meter of court on reflex — no one's forcing him back yet, and that's the tell to watch.

BIOMECHANICS: Warmup-phase exchange, Player A just exited a ~766 ms rally (19.3s → 20.07s). Court Coverage has collapsed to 0.16 m/s this window vs a 0.33 m/s opening baseline — a 50.75% drop (z=-0.76). Court Position is stable-to-improving: retreat peaked at 0.78 m around t=12.3s but Player A has reclaimed ground, now sitting at 0.17 m with a trend slope of -0.03 m/s. Recovery Lag has only a single sample (33 ms) — no fatigue read yet.

STRATEGY: Don't confuse low Court Coverage (-50.75%) with laziness — these are sub-second warmup touches, not real rallies. The usable signal is Court Position: Player A's 0.78 m retreat on rally #2 then snap-back to 0.17 m shows he's actively testing depth tolerance. Lock in that forward stance as the match baseline; any drift past 0.35 m once live points start is an early defensive-posture flag to act on immediately.

NARRATIVE: Player A is calibrating footwork in warmup — holding the baseline tight and saving his legs for when it counts.

BIOMECHANICS: Just-ended rally was A's longest of the match (~5.0s vs prior ~1-2s bursts). Court Position spiked to 1.95 m behind the baseline — 2.5× any prior rally this match (previous max 0.78 m), with a +0.06 m/s drift slope across the session. Court Coverage simultaneously jumped to 0.59 m/s lateral, the highest reading logged and more than double the prior rally mean (0.21 m/s).

STRATEGY: The moment rallies extend past three shots, A's Court Position collapses backward — 1.95 m of retreat under only 0.59 m/s lateral load means he's ceding depth before he's even been stretched wide. A needs to step up and take the ball early on the next return; the retreat slope (+0.06 m/s sustained) says his default reset position is drifting deep, and from 2 m behind the baseline his angles die. Shorten the strike zone, rob the opponent of recovery time.

NARRATIVE: First extended rally of the match and A already gave up two meters of court — he's defending from the fence on point one.

BIOMECHANICS: Player A's Court Position is the tell this rally — retreat distance ramped 0.17 m → 0.04 m → 1.95 m → 0.99 m across the last four rally samples (trend +0.15 m/s, current-window mean 0.79 m vs 0.62 m baseline, +27%). Court Coverage also ticked up: lateral 95th-pct 0.44 m/s, +33% over baseline. Recovery Lag is silent this window (no post-rally decel sample yet).

STRATEGY: A got pushed off the baseline mid-rally (1.95 m retreat spike) and only partially recovered ground before the point ended. Next service point, A must anchor inside the baseline on the split — Court Position drift of +27% with a positive slope means one more deep ball and he's hitting on the run again. Shorten the swing, take the ball earlier; don't let the rally reach sample four.

NARRATIVE: Player A is getting walked backwards — every rally this game he's ceding another meter of court, and the legs are starting to cover it.

narrator_beats.text: Player A settles into the baseline, shoulders dropping as breath steadies before the first point.
Player A's shoulders drop slightly—breath catching before the next serve.
Player A's ball toss drifts five inches right of his baseline anchor—weight already shifting to compensate before his feet leave the court.
Player A's lateral shuffle slows—weight settling heavy on the outside foot, fatigue creeping into court coverage.
Player A's lateral push slows—weight settling deeper into each step, the quick-twitch rhythm breaking into heavier strides.
Player A's lateral shuffle slows—weight settling center court as the rally stretches into its third exchange.
